summary: Every visit row can change one employee-day total, so exact output has a linear inspection lower bound; one grouped scan matches that bound.

optimality.argument: An uninspected visit can be the only row for an employee-day pair or can change an existing pair's total duration. Every one of the R rows must therefore be considered. Computing one duration and updating one hash aggregate per row takes constant expected work, for O(R) total time.

replacement_checks.evidence: Changing the in_time, out_time, emp_id, or event_day of any uninspected row can alter a returned group or total.
The query scans each row once, computes one subtraction, and updates exactly one employee-day sum.
Authored fixtures cover the public sample, one visit, repeated visits, separate employees and dates, unsorted groups, minute boundaries, and one-minute intervals.
